scope.summary: Enumerate and wire the six MD-parity keyframeable fabric-property slots (Shrinkage Weft, Shrinkage Warp, Solidify, Pressure, Tack Strength, Trim Weight) as named Track<T> variants in the animation property registry, with per-substep GPU uniform upload for each.
acceptance_criteria: MaterialKeyframe enum has named variants: ShrinkageWeft(f32), ShrinkageWarp(f32), Solidify(f32), Pressure(f32), TackStrength(f32), TrimWeight(f32), each carrying a normalized [0,1] value.
Property registry (MT-281) lists all six as distinct entries with their Track<f32> resolved type.
Per-substep GPU uniform upload path uploads the interpolated value of each active track slot to the WGSL uniform buffer before the constraint solve for that substep.
A garment spec or animation JSON referencing any of the six named slots round-trips through tailor_animations without name loss.
The ANIMATION_STABLE descriptor (MT-297) catches NaN injection from an out-of-range keyframe value on any of the six slots.
scope.risk_if_missed: MOAT-4 (keyframeable physical properties) ships as a structural skeleton with no named per-property contract. Models and operators cannot target MD-equivalent animated inflatables, wet-drape drying, or dynamic stiffening because the six slots are not addressable by name. The MD-parity claim for keyframeable fabric dynamics is false.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
